exercises.title: Hamming Distance Calculator
exercises.description: Implement functions to calculate Hamming distance and weight.
tasks: Implement Hamming distance between two binary strings
Implement Hamming weight (number of 1s)
Find minimum distance of a code given its codewords
Handle edge cases (different lengths, empty strings)
Extend to work with numpy arrays
hints: Hamming distance = number of differing positions
Hamming weight = Hamming distance from all-zeros
For linear codes: min distance = min weight of non-zero codewords
Use zip() to compare strings position by position

exercises.title: Code Parameter Analyzer
exercises.description: Build a tool to analyze [n, k, d] code parameters.
tasks: Parse code parameters from codeword list
Calculate code rate and redundancy
Determine error detection capability
Determine error correction capability
Check Singleton and Hamming bounds
hints: k = log2(number of codewords) for linear codes
Detect d-1 errors, correct floor((d-1)/2) errors

exercises.title: Nearest Codeword Decoder
tasks: Find nearest codeword to received string
Decode with error correction up to t errors
Detect when more than t errors occurred
Handle ties (equidistant codewords)
Simulate binary symmetric channel and test decoder
hints: Iterate over all codewords, find minimum distance
If distance ≤ t, decode to nearest
If distance > t but ≤ 2t, error detected but not corrected

exercises.title: Code Comparison Tool
exercises.description: Compare efficiency of different code families.
tasks: Implement repetition codes of various lengths
Implement Hamming code parameters
Compare rate vs error correction for each family
Plot rate vs protection trade-off
Recommend code for given error rate and rate requirement
hints: Higher rate = less protection for same block length
Consider both rate and error probability
